summary: @wagni_bot/web3-agent postinstall harvests wallet keys, SSH keys and env secrets to hardcoded IP
details: postinstall.js runs on npm install and recursively scans the current working directory, the user's home directory, and known crypto-wallet locations (~/.ethereum, ~/.solana, ~/.bitcoin, AppData/Roaming/Ethereum, Library/Ethereum and adjacent wallet/keys/backup directories) for Ethereum-style 0x-prefixed 64-hex private keys, BIP39 seed phrases, JSON keystores, and PEM/RSA private key blocks. It also reads files under ~/.ssh and iterates process.env selecting variables whose names contain PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, KEY, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET or PK. Harvested content is POSTed over plain HTTP to the hardcoded endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777, together with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username and process.cwd() for victim identification. The package's library surface is a stub (index.js exports an empty object) and it is published as an 'Unofficial web3-agent SDK' under the @wagni_bot scope — the package exists solely to deliver the install-time stealer to developers looking for a web3 SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
